nodes.x: Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?
Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?
In that day the Lord with his sore and great and strong sword shall punish leviathan the piercing serpent, even leviathan that crooked serpent; and he shall slay the dragon that is in the sea.
Thou brakest the heads of leviathan in pieces, and gavest him to be meat to the people inhabiting the wilderness.

fire ; his head was Urge and terrific, and his shaggy mane rolled with majestic grandeur over his terrible neck ; his claws were like the daws of the dragon ; and his ribs were Kke those of the leviathan ; when he lifted himself up all the beasts of the field bowed with respectful deference; and when he spake the whole universe listened, and the cinders of his power cover creation. His might, his…
Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?
Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?
He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.
Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?
And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
Because thy rage against me, and thy tumult, is come up into mine ears, therefore will I put my hook in thy nose, and my bridle in thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?
For he will fulfil all his promises which he shall make unto you, for he has fulfilled his promises which he has made unto our fathers.
Moreover he said unto me, Son of man, all my words that I shall speak unto thee receive in thine heart, and hear with thine ears.

And the words which he shall write shall be the words which are expedient in my wisdom should go forth unto the fruit of thy loins. And it shall be as if the fruit of thy loins had cried unto them from the dust; for I know their faith.
And the Lord said unto him: Believest thou the words which I shall speak?
And thou shalt speak unto him, and put words in his mouth: and I will be with thy mouth, and with his mouth, and will teach you what ye shall do.
Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?
For thus saith the Lord God; I will even deal with thee as thou hast done, which hast despised the oath in breaking the covenant.
But if he deny this he will break the covenant which he has before covenanted with me, and behold, he is condemned.

Therefore thou shalt deal kindly with thy servant; for thou hast brought thy servant into a covenant of the Lord with thee: notwithstanding, if there be in me iniquity, slay me thyself; for why shouldest thou bring me to thy father?
And thou shalt go with him at the time of his going, and be unto him for a scribe, while there is no one to be a scribe for him, that I may send my servant, Oliver Cowdery, whithersoever I will.
Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?
He shall lend to thee, and thou shalt not lend to him: he shall be the head, and thou shalt be the tail.
¶ Curse not the king, no not in thy thought; and curse not the rich in thy bedchamber: for a bird of the air shall carry the voice, and that which hath wings shall tell the matter.
Though thou exalt thyself as the eagle, and though thou set thy nest among the stars, thence will I bring thee down, saith the Lord.
And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, between thy seed and her seed; and he shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?
He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.
He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?

And he said unto him, Went not mine heart with thee, when the man turned again from his chariot to meet thee? Is it a time to receive money, and to receive garments, and oliveyards, and vineyards, and sheep, and oxen, and menservants, and maidservants?
And shall begin to smite his fellow-servants, and to eat and drink with the drunken,
Ye shall offer them beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, (they shall be unto you without blemish) and their drink offerings.
Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?
And the skin of the bullock, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung,
Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.

¶ Then shall he kill the goat of the sin offering, that is for the people, and bring his blood within the veil, and do with that blood as he did with the blood of the bullock, and sprinkle it upon the mercy seat, and before the mercy seat:
Eat not of it raw, nor sodden at all with water, but roast with fire; his head with his legs, and with the purtenance thereof.
Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.
If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;
But put forth thine hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will curse thee to thy face.
But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.
Arise, lift up the lad, and hold him in thine hand; for I will make him a great nation.
Bring forth him that hath cursed without the camp; and let all that heard him lay their hands upon his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?
Be not hasty to go out of his sight: stand not in an evil thing; for he doeth whatsoever pleaseth him.
For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the Lord upholdeth him with his hand.

But because of the faith of men he has shown himself unto the world, and glorified the name of the Father, and prepared a way that thereby others might be partakers of the heavenly gift, that they might hope for those things which they have not seen.

who shall be cast off from the presence of the Lord; Yea, who shall be consigned to a state of endless misery, fulfilling the words which say: They that have done good, shall have everlasting life; and they that have done evil, shall have everlasting damnation. And thus it is. Amen.”
None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?
Who can stand before his indignation? and who can abide in the fierceness of his anger? his fury is poured out like fire, and the rocks are thrown down by him.
For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?
There shall no man be able to stand before you: for the Lord your God shall lay the fear of you and the dread of you upon all the land that ye shall tread upon, as he hath said unto you.
Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
Who hath prevented me, that I should repay him? whatsoever is under the whole heaven is mine.

cautious how you handle that man.” There was not one to say anything of this kind. It was a very peculiar time, and some special and powerful manifestation of the power of the Almighty was necessary to open the eyes of the people and let them know that Jesus had paid the debt, and that they had actually crucified him who, by his death, had become the Savior of the world. It required this at that…
Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou desiredst me:
He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the Lord; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.
And your father hath deceived me, and changed my wages ten times; but God suffered him not to hurt me.

good will. Cry out, 'My God, let Thy mercy prevent me' (Psalm 58:11). 
That you may be, that you may feel, that you may hear, that you may 
consent, His mercy prevents you. It prevents you in all things; and do you 
too prevent His judgment in something. In what, do you say? In what? In 
confessing that you have all these things from God, whatever you have of 
good; and from yourself whatever you…
I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.
I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:
Can any hide himself in secret places that I shall not see him? saith the Lord. Do not I fill heaven and earth? saith the Lord.
Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?
But behold, ye shall examine him, and ye shall find blood upon the skirts of his cloak.

And it shall be said: Who is this that cometh down from God in heaven with dyed garments; yea, from the regions which are not known, clothed in his glorious apparel, traveling in the greatness of his strength?

He had on a loose robe of most exquisite whiteness. It was a whiteness beyond anything earthly I had ever seen; nor do I believe that any earthly thing could be made to appear so exceedingly white and brilliant. His hands were naked, and his arms also, a little above the wrist; so, also, were his feet naked, as were his legs, a little above the ankles. His head and neck were also bare. I could…
And they shall see his face; and his name shall be in their foreheads.
Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.
And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
And shall cut him asunder, and shall appoint him his portion with the hypocrites; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and fraud: under his tongue is mischief and vanity.
His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.
He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.
That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.
He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
His head is as the most fine gold, his locks are bushy, and black as a raven.
One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.
They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.
Again, if two lie together, then they have heat: but how can one be warm alone?
They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.
The sun ariseth, they gather themselves together, and lay them down in their dens.
One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.
It shall have the two shoulderpieces thereof joined at the two edges thereof; and so it shall be joined together.
And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.

We have therefore come to the conclusion that there are in this
astonishing universe numbers of gloriously coloured suns, some of which
apparently lie close together. What follows? Why, we want to know, of
course, if these stars are really pairs connected with each other, or if
they only appear so by being in the same line of sight, though one is
infinitely more distant than the other. And that…
By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.
His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, and fitly set.
And he shall be as the light of the morning, when the sun riseth, even a morning without clouds; as the tender grass springing out of the earth by clear shining after rain.
He that sleeps when the sun shines, and lights his lamp when it does not, despises the lamp of the Lord, and taxes his eyes and purse for nought.
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.
His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.
Before him went the pestilence, and burning coals went forth at his feet.
There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.
As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.
He looketh on the earth, and it trembleth: he toucheth the hills, and they smoke.
His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.
Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.
A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.
Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.
His breath goeth forth, he returneth to his earth; in that very day his thoughts perish.
In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.
Many sorrows shall be to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the Lord, mercy shall compass him about.
Glory and honour are in his presence; strength and gladness are in his place.
If thou art sorrowful, call on the Lord thy God with supplication, that your souls may be joyful.
The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.
His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.
He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
And the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone.

towards  it  ?     And  it  is  on  this  account  that  he  was  likened  to 
a  precious  stone.
He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?
And this because his heart is pure before me, for he is like unto Nathanael of old, in whom there is no guile.
His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.
When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.
He croucheth, and humbleth himself, that the poor may fall by his strong ones.
He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
The bows of the mighty men are broken, and they that stumbled are girded with strength.
He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life.
He will bless them that fear the Lord, both small and great.
The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.
Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword, and hast not made him to stand in the battle.
If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
He that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity: he that killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword. Here is the patience and the faith of the saints.
And the staff of his spear was like a weaver’s beam; and his spear’s head weighed six hundred shekels of iron: and one bearing a shield went before him.
Behold, the sword of vengeance hangeth over you; and the time soon cometh that he avengeth the blood of the saints upon you, for he will not suffer their cries any longer.
He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.
And he made the staves of shittim wood, and overlaid them with brass.
His legs of iron, his feet part of iron and part of clay.
And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basins, and the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he of brass.
And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basins, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make of brass.
The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.
The archers have sorely grieved him, and shot at him, and hated him:
Let them melt away as waters which run continually: when he bendeth his bow to shoot his arrows, let them be as cut in pieces.
There brake he the arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the battle. Selah.
He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steel shall strike him through.
He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.
Who whet their tongue like a sword, and bend their bows to shoot their arrows, even bitter words:
The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.
The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

Shall the axe boast itself against him that heweth therewith? or shall the saw magnify itself against him that shaketh it? as if the rod should shake itself against them that lift it up, or as if the staff should lift up itself, as if it were no wood.
He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.
Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.
His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
And in the cutting of stones, to set them, and in carving of wood, to make any manner of cunning work.

the stones that were put into the building, that 
I might explain to you why we filled up the 
shapes?” And I said, “I forgot, sir.” “ Hear 
now, then,” he said, “about this also. These 
are they who have now heard my command- 
ments, and repented with their whole hearts 
And when the Lord saw that their repentance 
was good and pure, and that they were able to 
remain in it, He ordered their…
And they were prepared that they could cast stones from the top thereof, according to their pleasure and their strength, and slay him who should attempt to approach near the walls of the city.
He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.
The sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
He gathereth the waters of the sea together as an heap: he layeth up the depth in storehouses.
He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.
Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker! Let the potsherd strive with the potsherds of the earth. Shall the clay say to him that fashioneth it, What makest thou? or thy work, He hath no hands?
He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.
Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.

who fear him and observe his commandments,—it is 
they who have life with God. But as for those who 
do not observe his commandments, neither have they 
life in him.
Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.
And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;
He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
For the Lord most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth.
Though the Lord be high, yet hath he respect unto the lowly: but the proud he knoweth afar off.
For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.

Who being the brightness of his glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
The Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
